summary: Video covers Python local development setup using venv and pyproject.toml. Emphasizes activating virtual environments before installing and avoiding system/project Python mixing.

methods_tools_patterns.name: venv for virtual environments
methods_tools_patterns.description: Use python -m venv .venv to create, then activate before installing
methods_tools_patterns.evidence: I recommend venv for most cases. You run python -m venv .venv and then activate it.

methods_tools_patterns.name: pyproject.toml for dependencies
methods_tools_patterns.description: Preferred over requirements.txt for structured dependency management
methods_tools_patterns.evidence: pyproject.toml for dependency management. It's becoming the standard.

key_claims.claim: venv is recommended for most Python projects
key_claims.evidence: I recommend venv for most cases

open_questions: What specific pyproject.toml structure does the video recommend?
